other_observers: none; I found the bird and reported it on WSBN. The next day Art Dahl from Rifle was able to refind the bird at the same location
specific_location: The ibis was about 1 mile north of Harvey Gap SP at a small farm pond just south of Garfield 226 RD
description: Large dark bird with very long down-curved bill and long legs. As I was looking through the ibis flock one ibis clearly had thin, blue lines extending from the eye to the upper mandible and to the lower mandible. Upon further inspection of the ibis the eyes were brown, the lores were dark and the legs were gray. The blue lines stopped at the bill and did not extend behind the eye The knees were only slightly reddish.Behaviors: About 30 ibis were feeding along the edge of a small farm pond probing in the mud. All were White-faced Ibis except for the one I am reporting.Call: None heardPlumage: Adult breeding
